channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya to compensate absentee landowners in Tana River and issue title deeds to long-term settlers
item.description: President William Ruto has announced a government plan aimed at resolving long-standing land ownership disputes in Tana River County by compensating absentee landlords and issuing title deeds to resid...

item.title: Tanzania maintains centralised system for national ID card printing amid decentralisation debate
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has confirmed that it will continue using a centralised system for printing national identity cards, despite ongoing discussions and pressure for decentralisation. The annou...

item.title: Comelec to work with Ombudsman on possible plunder and indirect bribery cases vs Marcoleta
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has expressed its readiness to coordinate with the Office of the Ombudsman regarding reported cases involving alleged plunder and indirect bribery filed against f...

item.title: Why Africa’s financial growth depends more on infrastructure than apps
item.description: Obinna Chukwujioke, a Nigerian fintech entrepreneur and infrastructure builder, explains why Africa’s long-term economic growth will depend more on strong financial infrastructure than just consumer-f...

item.title: Oleksandr Usyk to defend WBC heavyweight title against Rico Verhoeven in boxing event at the Pyramids of Giza
item.description: Oleksandr Usyk is scheduled to face Dutch kickboxing champion Rico Verhoeven in a heavyweight boxing match taking place at the pyramids of Giza in Egypt. The bout will be contested under standard boxi...

item.title: Comelec says no motion filed yet to reopen Marcoleta campaign donation investigation
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) said it currently has no plans to revive the previously dismissed investigation involving Senator Rodante Marcoleta’s campaign donations during the 2025 elections...

item.title: Experts share key camping safety tips for Canadians heading outdoors this summer
item.description: A CTV News Ottawa article by Adam Beauchemin highlights important safety advice for Canadians planning camping trips during the summer season. The report focuses on practical precautions that campers ...

item.title: King Charles III attends Royal Shakespeare Company's The Tempest performance
item.description: King Charles III made an unexpected appearance at a sold-out performance of The Tempest by the Royal Shakespeare Company at the Royal Shakespeare Theatre in Stratford-upon-Avon. The monarch received a...

item.title: Claudia Cardinale Portraits and Behind-the-Scenes Stills from the 1962 Film ‘Cartouche’
item.description: The article highlights a collection of vintage portraits of actress Claudia Cardinale taken during the production of the 1962 French adventure film ‘Cartouche,’ directed by Philippe de Broca. The film...

item.title: Clashes in KP’s Bannu leave 12 militants dead, one civilian killed; police defuse bomb
item.description: Heavy fighting broke out in the Barakzai Akhundkhel area of Bannu district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, where police engaged militants in a violent exchange of fire. According to senior police officials, at...

item.title: FG Launches SEED Programme To Train 6,000 Power Sector Professionals Across Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government don launch the Siemens Energy Education and Development (SEED) training facility for Abeokuta under the Presidential Power Initiative (PPI), with plans to train at least 6,000 e...

item.title: Ugandan official accused of defrauding Chinese investors of 1.6 billion shillings
item.description: Reports indicate a fraud case involving approximately 1.6 billion Ugandan shillings allegedly linked to Chinese investors, raising concerns about investment security in East Africa. According to the i...

item.title: Three months into Iran conflict, questions grow over US strategy and outcomes
item.description: Three months after the United States launched military action against Iran under President Donald Trump, questions are mounting over whether Washington is achieving its strategic objectives, despite e...

item.title: Konstantin Ukhtomsky and the Architectural Watercolors of Imperial Russia
item.description: Konstantin Ukhtomsky (1818–1881) was a distinguished Russian painter and watercolorist renowned for his refined architectural views and interior scenes of imperial palaces. Serving as an official arti...

item.title: Civil group demands urgent financial recovery plan from City of Johannesburg amid Eskom debt crisis
item.description: The City of Johannesburg is under increasing pressure to urgently address its deepening financial crisis after civil society organisation Public Interest SA issued a formal demand letter giving the mu...

item.title: Inquiry finds nine hospital staff responsible for surgery without HIV screening at Nishtar Hospital Multan
item.description: An inquiry committee formed by Nishtar Medical University has concluded that nine doctors and hospital officials were responsible for serious negligence and violation of medical protocols in a case in...

item.title: 82 confirmed dead and several trapped after gas explosion at Shanxi coal mine in China
item.description: A catastrophic gas explosion occurred at the Liushenyu coal mine in Changzhi city, Shanxi province, northern China, on Friday evening, resulting in the deaths of at least 82 workers, with dozens more ...

item.title: Former university student surviving on waste collection gifted furnished home after viral story
item.description: A young man identified as Haji, who was forced to drop out of university due to financial difficulties, has received a fresh start after being gifted a fully furnished house by a well-wisher. After le...

item.title: Jahangir’s Philosophical Engagement with Vedanta and Sufism
item.description: In the winter of 1617, the Mughal emperor Jahangir undertook a remarkable spiritual journey that demonstrated his deep intellectual curiosity. He personally travelled to Ujjain to meet Jadrup Gosain, ...

item.title: EU accelerates push for digital sovereignty amid growing tensions with US tech companies
item.description: European governments are increasingly moving to reduce their dependence on US technology companies, driven by geopolitical tensions, concerns about data sovereignty, and fears over political and legal...
